content.full_text:
January 27, 1988


Mark Kempton
928 - 45th Street
Sacramento, CA 95819

                                    Re:  Your Request for Advice
                                         Our File No I-87-328

Dear Mr. Kempton:

     You have requested advice concerning the permissible use of
surplus campaign funds.

     The Fair Political Practices Commission interprets and
enforces the Political Reform Act of 1974.  (Government Code
Sections 81000-91015, copy enclosed.)  The Political Reform Act
does not address the permissible uses of campaign funds.  However,
enclosed for your information is a copy of California Elections
Code Section 12400, et seq.  The California Attorney General
interprets the Elections Code.  If you have questions concerning
Elections Code Section 12400, et seq., please call Ted Prim, Deputy
Attorney General, at (916) 324-5481.


     If you have any questions about this letter, you may call me
at (916) 322-5662.

                                    Sincerely,

                                    Diane M. Griffiths
                                    General Counsel


                                    By  Jeanne Pritchard
                                        Division Chief
                                        Technical Assistance and
                                        Analysis Division

December 25, 2987

Fair Political Practices Commission
Sacramento, California

Gentlemen:

Years ago I was the Treasurer for "Randy Burton for SMUD". According to bank records (see enclosed) the campaign has a balance of $39.27. The bank is charging $1.00 per month to service this inactive account because the balance is less than $200.00.

What must I do with this balance to be within the law? While the balance is very small, I don't want to get the system upset by breaking one of the many complex rules that exist today.

I see these as some of the options:

1. return the funds to the candidate;

2. contribute the amount to a charity;

3. let the bank service charge the account out out of existence; and

4. give the money to the state (ugh).

Please advise as soon as possible what I can do legally.

Sincerely,

Mark Kempton
928 45th St.
Sacramento, California 95819

JMK T D:H JO
LJR D/S N:J MAB
MPM L C-Z —
LSK

CapFed
Capital Federal
Savings and Loan Association

DEC 2 1 1987

December 16, 1987

Randy Burton for SMUD
Campaign Committee
C/O Mark Kempton
555 Capitol Mall Ste 1235
Sacramento CA 95814

Re: Account # 282361

Dear Mr. Kempton:

The enclosed letter regarding verification of your Tax Identification
number is being returned for your signature. The hand written note
that was placed at the top of the letter indicates that you are under
the impression that the account has been closed. According to our
records the account has a current balance of $39.27. The type of
account these funds are in has a $1.00 per month service charge if
the balance is under $200.00.

Please come into our office to bring the balance up to $200.00 or to
close the account since it has been inactive for some time. If you
have any questions, please contact us at 923-4501.

Sincerely yours,

JUDY ANN RANDALL
Sales Representative

P.O. Box 13326 • Sacramento, CA 95813 • (916) 920-3333

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


December 31, 1987


Mark Kempton
928 - 45th Street
Sacramento, CA  95819

Re: 87-328

Dear Mr. Kempton:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on December 29, 1987 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to the information needed. If your request is
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we
can. (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec.
18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Jeanne Pritchard
Chief
Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: What are the permissible uses of surplus campaign funds under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC determined that the Political Reform Act does not govern surplus campaign fund usage, and referred the requester to the California Elections Code sections 12400 et seq., which are interpreted by the California Attorney General rather than the FPPC.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document is multi-part containing an initial acknowledgment letter, an unrelated savings and loan bank letter, and the substantive FPPC response. The advisory portion is clear and well-structured. Minor OCR quality issues noted in the S&L letter which is not part of the FPPC response.

CONCLUSION: The Political Reform Act does not address the permissible uses of campaign funds. However, enclosed for your information is a copy of California Elections Code section 12400, et seg. The California Attorney General interprets the Elections Code.

embedding.summary: This file contains FPPC correspondence regarding surplus campaign fund usage, including an acknowledgment letter dated December 31, 1987, and a substantive response dated January 27, 1988, clarifying that the Political Reform Act does not address this matter and referencing Elections Code provisions instead.
